introSection: Kumba is a steel roller coaster currently located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. At the time of its opening in 1993, it had the record for the most inversions on a roller coaster and tallest vertical loop until 1995, when Dragon Khan opened at PortAventura Park. The ride was built by Bolliger & Mabillard.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In November 1992, Busch Gardens Tampa announced the addition of Kumba.

Kumba opened on April 20, 1993. It was the first roller coaster with a dive loop and interlocking corkscrews.

Kumba reportedly closed on November 21, 2024 and remained standing but not operating. The park announced on April 21, 2025 that the ride would reopen in the summer. Kumba was seen testing on August 15.

On September 23, the park announced that Kumba would reopen for passholders on September 24 and to the general public on September 27.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
Kumba features a pre-drop, a small drop designed by Bolliger & Mabillard to reduce strain on the chain lift.

Kumba contains the first dive loop and interlocking corkscrews on a Bolliger & Mabillard roller coaster.

The lift hill passes through the middle of the vertical loop.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: After leaving the station, the train makes a right-hand, 90-degree turn and begins to climb the tall chain lift hill. At the top of the lift hill, the train makes a small pre-drop. The train then goes down a drop to the left, after this, the train enters the first inversion a  tall vertical loop which wraps around the lift hill. After the vertical loop, the train enters the next inversion a dive loop. After the dive loop, the train enters a third inversion a zero-g roll. After the zero-g roll, the train begins to enter the fourth inversion, a cobra roll. After it exits the cobra roll, the train enters a mid-course brake run. After the mid-course brake run, the train enters some interlocking corkscrews. The train enters the tunnel before upward clockwise helix and hits the final brakes, before making a right-hand turn and returning to the station.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Kumba has a light blue track and maroon supports. The ride received a fresh coat of paint for the 2019 season from Baynum Painting.

sections.title: Name
sections.content: Kumba means Roar in the African Congo Language. When the train traverses the track, it makes a very loud roaring sound. This is caused by the hollow square-shaped track.

fullText:
Kumba is a steel roller coaster currently located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. At the time of its opening in 1993, it had the record for the most inversions on a roller coaster and tallest vertical loop until 1995, when Dragon Khan opened at PortAventura Park. The ride was built by Bolliger & Mabillard.

In November 1992, Busch Gardens Tampa announced the addition of Kumba.

Kumba opened on April 20, 1993. It was the first roller coaster with a dive loop and interlocking corkscrews.

Kumba reportedly closed on November 21, 2024 and remained standing but not operating. The park announced on April 21, 2025 that the ride would reopen in the summer. Kumba was seen testing on August 15.

On September 23, the park announced that Kumba would reopen for passholders on September 24 and to the general public on September 27.

Kumba features a pre-drop, a small drop designed by Bolliger & Mabillard to reduce strain on the chain lift.

Kumba contains the first dive loop and interlocking corkscrews on a Bolliger & Mabillard roller coaster.

The lift hill passes through the middle of the vertical loop.

After leaving the station, the train makes a right-hand, 90-degree turn and begins to climb the tall chain lift hill. At the top of the lift hill, the train makes a small pre-drop. The train then goes down a drop to the left, after this, the train enters the first inversion a  tall vertical loop which wraps around the lift hill. After the vertical loop, the train enters the next inversion a dive loop. After the dive loop, the train enters a third inversion a zero-g roll. After the zero-g roll, the train begins to enter the fourth inversion, a cobra roll. After it exits the cobra roll, the train enters a mid-course brake run. After the mid-course brake run, the train enters some interlocking corkscrews. The train enters the tunnel before upward clockwise helix and hits the final brakes, before making a right-hand turn and returning to the station.

Kumba has a light blue track and maroon supports. The ride received a fresh coat of paint for the 2019 season from Baynum Painting.



Kumba means Roar in the African Congo Language. When the train traverses the track, it makes a very loud roaring sound. This is caused by the hollow square-shaped track.
